name: Arthur Koestler
abstract: Arthur Koestler  (UK: , US: ; German: [ˈaʁtuːɐ̯ ˈkœstlɐ]; Hungarian: Kösztler Artúr [ˈkøstlɛr ˈɒrtuːr]; 5 September 1905 – 1 March 1983) was an Austro-Hungarian-born author and journalist. Koestler was born in Budapest, and was educated in Austria, apart from his early school years.

about_quotes.speaker: David N. Baker
about_quotes.evidence: It’s by the guy who wrote the book Darkness at Noon, Arthur Koestler
about_quotes.doc_title: David N. Baker — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Bill Dobbins
about_quotes.evidence: I think he wrote it around ’89, late 80s or early 90s — one is that the evolutionary process — and this is an idea also that was explored a little bit by Arthur Koestler, a really brilliant novelist and humanist and writer who was born in Budapest and grew up and was educated in England.
about_quotes.doc_title: Bill Dobbins interviewed by Monk Rowe, Rochester, New York, January 11, 2012

about_quotes.speaker: Hentoff Nat
about_quotes.evidence: I had recently read at the age of 15 Arthur Koestler’s Darkness at Noon
about_quotes.doc_title: Hentoff Nat — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
